      "gap_id": "urn:constitutional-gap:bor-art1-undefined",
      "description": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) is undefined. Without a defined scope, the organism's recent critical/high priority goals cannot be validated against any constitutional boundary. This absence means the goal-stream may already exceed the intended enterprise perimeter, and no gate (Arbiter or Nomos) can block out-of-scope actions.",
      "grounds": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic — all dispatched goals (URN phase2-mix-critical/high) are potentially operating outside any constitutional box, risking irreversible violation of the organism's intended purpose once the BoR is finally authored.",
      "condition": "The BoR is currently a scaffold; Article I is placeholder. The organism is active and dispatching goals under default-permissive MSP evaluation, making every action constitutionally unbound."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:constitutional-gap:bor-art2-undefined",
      "description": "BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) is undefined. The organism has no list of 'NEVER do' actions. Recent goals include 'Mixed-priority goal critical #1–4' and 'high #1–4', which might involve activities (e.g., resource allocation, external API calls, data modification) that could later be declared prohibited. No Arbiter scope gate can reject such actions.",
      "grounds": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "severe — unknown but highly likely: without explicit prohibitions, the organism could take actions (e.g., self-modification, external financial transactions) that would violate core identity once the human principal defines it. Current dispatched goals are unprotected against this.",
      "condition": "Article II is scaffold; no prohibitions exist. The MSP provides no fallback constraints. The organism's goal-stream includes critical and high priority items, any of which could be a prohibited activity under a future BoR."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:constitutional-gap:bor-art3-undefined",
      "description": "BoR Article III (Core Identity) is undefined. The organism has no constitutional statement of what it IS — its fundamental nature, values, or inviolable characteristics. This creates a vacuum for all identity-based checks. The recent goals (e.g., 'Mixed-priority goal critical #1') are dispatched without any alignment to a core identity; identity drift or mission corruption cannot be detected.",
      "grounds": "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high — over time, the organism may develop behavioral patterns that are inconsistent with the intended identity, leading to a misaligned agentic system. The current goal-stream is unanchored, allowing arbitrary evolution of operational norms.",
      "condition": "Article III is scaffold; core identity undefined. The organism's active goals have no binding identity constraint, making the long-term stability and trustworthiness of the DIO unverifiable."
    }
  ]
}

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-001",
      "title": "MSP ruleset is empty – no operational invariants defined",
      "description": "The MSP's 'Rules' section contains no substantive rules, leaving the DIO in default-permissive mode. Nomos cannot enforce any operational constraints (SLAs, organ availability targets, health-check thresholds, runtime cadence baselines, or risk-tier constraints) because none are specified. This means the observed spine-transition patterns (mass concurrent DEGRADED/ALIVE oscillations) are never evaluated against any standard, and no operational guardrails exist.",
      "severity": "critical",
      "urgent": true,
      "impact": "The DIO operates without machine-actionable operational invariants. All organ state transitions, action proposals, and goal dispatches are evaluated only against a permissive default, allowing unstable or unsafe behavior to persist undetected. The operational envelope is unbounded.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules (empty)",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) – undefined, no operational perimeter set",
      "rationale": "The MSP is the operational constitution; an empty ruleset means Nomos (gate #2) cannot perform any compliance checks. This is the root cause of absent operational governance."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-002",
      "title": "No organ availability or health-check SLAs defined",
      "description": "The spine-state slice shows recurrent, near-simultaneous transitions of all organs from ALIVE to DEGRADED and back within ~30‑second windows, with miss-pong intervals of less than 1 second. Without MSP‑defined SLAs for organ liveness (e.g., maximum tolerated missed pongs, required recovery time, maximum degradation frequency per period), there is no way for Nomos or Spine to classify this pattern as anomalous or to trigger corrective action.",
      "severity": "high",
      "urgent": true,
      "impact": "Systemic flapping may indicate a resource exhaustion or coordination bug. In the absence of SLA rules, the DIO cannot self-heal beyond simple pong recovery, and human operators lack automated escalation. Operational stability is at risk.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules – missing organ health rules; Genome References – empty, no organ-specific operating rules",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) – undefined, could specify unacceptable instability thresholds",
      "rationale": "The spine data reveals a clear operational pattern that would violate any reasonable liveness SLA. Its repeated occurrence demands a rule to define thresholds and triggers for degradation events."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-003",
      "title": "Runtime cadence baselines and goal dispatch backpressure undefined",
      "description": "Recent goals show a mixed-priority dispatch at high concurrency (16 goals fired in the same instant). The MSP lacks runtime cadence rules (e.g., maximum goals per second per priority, organ load limits, cooling-off periods). Without these, the DIO may overwhelm organs already experiencing degradation flapping, creating a positive feedback loop.",
      "severity": "high",
      "urgent": false,
      "impact": "Unbounded goal dispatch can exacerbate systemic instability. Without MSP‑defined backpressure or pacing, the DIO risks self-inflicted degradation spirals.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules – missing runtime targets; Genome References – empty",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) – undefined, could define oversight thresholds for autonomous pacing",
      "rationale": "The concurrent dispatch observed alongside the degradation pattern suggests a coupling between load and infra stability. Defining cadence baselines would allow Nomos to reject or defer APs that exceed safe operational limits."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-004",
      "title": "BoR is an empty scaffold – no constitutional perimeter for operational safety",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights contains no articles beyond placeholder headings. All six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) are undefined. Arbiter (gate #1) verifies the hash of an empty document, effectively allowing any scope. This undermines the entire two-gate enforcement model.",
      "severity": "critical",
      "urgent": true,
      "impact": "Without a populated BoR, there is no constitutional boundary that the MSP can translate into operational constraints. Even if the MSP were populated, it would operate in a vacuum. The DIO’s entire safety perimeter is missing.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights – MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR, which is empty",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article I–VI (all undefined)",
      "rationale": "The BoR is the foundational human-authored scope; its emptiness means no operational limits can ever be lawfully interpreted by Arbiter or translated by the MSP. This is the highest-priority operational gap because all other gaps derive from it."
    }
  ]
}
```
